title: Martin Chuzzlewit
description: Martin Chuzzlewit — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "Martin Chuzzlewit" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Alcoholism
memes.description: The motif of excessive drinking in Martin Chuzzlewit, from Sairey Gamp's notorious attachment to the bottle to the broader theme of moral weakness expressed through intoxication.

memes.label: America
memes.description: The United States of America as depicted and satirised in Martin Chuzzlewit — the setting for Martin's disillusioning journey abroad.

memes.label: The Anglo-Bengalee
memes.description: The Anglo-Bengalee Disinterested Loan and Life Assurance Company, Montague Tigg's fraudulent City enterprise — Dickens's great satirical anatomy of Victorian speculation, ornamental respectability, and financial imposture.
aliases: Anglo-Bengalee Disinterested Loan and Life Assurance Company

memes.label: Anthony Chuzzlewit
memes.description: Anthony Chuzzlewit, brother of Old Martin Chuzzlewit and father of Jonas, a miserly Manchester warehouseman whose cunning and covetousness he passed on to his son.

memes.label: Appearance
memes.description: The central Dickensian theme of appearance versus reality in Martin Chuzzlewit — a novel in which every character's outward presentation is a deliberate construction, and the art of reading through surfaces is the reader's essential skill

memes.label: Architecture
memes.description: The profession of architecture in Martin Chuzzlewit, satirised through Seth Pecksniff's fraudulent practice and contrasted with Martin Chuzzlewit the Younger's genuine aspirations (005-002).

memes.label: Augustus Moddle
memes.description: The youngest gentleman boarder at Todgers's — a melodramatically melancholy young man who becomes engaged to Charity Pecksniff as "Victim Number One," described by Dickens as a man upon whom the sun has set, the billows have rolled, and the deadly Upas tree of Java has blighted. (032-024)

memes.label: Augustus Moddle
memes.description: A melancholy young gentleman boarder at Todgers's who becomes the hapless object of Charity Pecksniff's matrimonial campaign.

memes.label: Austin Friars
memes.description: The London address where Tom Pinch finds mysterious, well-paid employment after his dismissal by Pecksniff — an office whose very obscurity is the clue to its true benefactor.

memes.label: "The Autumn Sunset at Salisbury — Chapter II Opening"
memes.description: The opening paragraphs of Chapter II — Dickens's celebrated description of an autumn sunset over the Wiltshire village near Salisbury, demonstrating his mastery of landscape prose and atmospheric scene-setting.
aliases: declining sun struggling through the mist

memes.label: Bailey Junior
memes.description: The cheeky boy-servant at Mrs. Todgers's boarding house, a comic Cockney character who later becomes a medical student — Dickens's portrait of irrepressible youthful vitality.

memes.label: Betsey Prig
memes.description: A hospital nurse and Mrs. Gamp's professional rival and occasional partner, whose "beard" and deep voice make her one of Dickens's most grotesque comic creations.

memes.label: Mr. Bevan
memes.description: Redirect to Mr. Bevan, the American gentleman who befriends and assists Martin Chuzzlewit during his journey to Eden.

memes.label: Bullamy
memes.description: The porter and doorkeeper at the Anglo-Bengalee Disinterested Loan and Life Assurance Company, Montague Tigg's obsequious servant who flees with the company's plunder (027-051).

relations.label: Anglo-Bengalee Disinterested Loan and Life Assurance Company

memes.label: Captain Kedgick
memes.description: The landlord of the National Hotel in the American settlement where Martin Chuzzlewit and Mark Tapley stay before their journey to Eden — a character whose flattery of his guests conceals a sharp commercial instinct.

memes.label: Charity Pecksniff
memes.description: The elder Pecksniff daughter, proud and calculating like her father, whose schemes for marriage come to nothing and who ends the novel bitter and alone.

memes.label: Chevy Slyme
memes.description: Chevy Slyme, a disreputable relation of the Chuzzlewit family and friend of Montague Tigg, whose defining trait is "always waiting round the corner" — the quintessential Dickensian sponger and schemer.

memes.label: Mr. Chuffey
memes.description: Anthony Chuzzlewit's ancient, semi-senile clerk whose unwavering loyalty and buried knowledge of Jonas's crimes make him the novel's unlikely vessel of truth.

memes.label: Class and Gentility
memes.description: Social class and the idea of gentility in Martin Chuzzlewit — Dickens's exploration of the distinction between true gentlemanliness and its hollow performance, a theme running through every stratum of the novel's society.

memes.label: Class and Gentility
memes.description: Dickens's satirical treatment of class pretension, gentility, and social climbing in Martin Chuzzlewit.

memes.label: Colonel Diver
memes.description: American newspaper editor encountered by Martin Chuzzlewit, a representative of the sensationalist American press.

memes.label: Comedy
memes.description: Dickens's use of comic techniques — caricature, verbal wit, ironic narration — in Martin Chuzzlewit, from the grotesque Sairey Gamp to the satirical American episodes.

memes.label: Commerce
memes.description: Commerce and business in Martin Chuzzlewit — from Pecksniff's fraudulent architecture to Tigg's Anglo-Bengalee swindle.

memes.label: Concealment
memes.description: Redirect to deception — the theme of concealment in Martin Chuzzlewit.

memes.label: Conscience
memes.description: A moral faculty systematically perverted in Martin Chuzzlewit — Dickens shows how conscience, meant to be the inner guide to right action, becomes in Pecksniff's hands a tool of self-congratulation and in Jonas's a tortured instrument of punishment.

memes.label: Constancy
memes.description: The virtue of steadfast loyalty and unwavering commitment, embodied in Tom Pinch's devotion and contrasted with the fickleness and betrayal that pervade Martin Chuzzlewit.

memes.label: Courtship
memes.description: The theme of courtship and marriage negotiations in Martin Chuzzlewit, explored through multiple contrasting couples — from Martin and Mary's tested love to Jonas Chuzzlewit's commodity marriage.

memes.label: Crime
memes.description: The evolution of crime in Martin Chuzzlewit — from Jonas Chuzzlewit's attempted patricide and successful fraud to the murder of Tigg Montague, tracing how selfishness, when unchecked, escalates into criminal violence.

memes.label: Cruelty
memes.description: The infliction of suffering through selfish action, manifested in Jonas Chuzzlewit's violence, Pecksniff's emotional manipulation, and the systemic cruelties Dickens exposes in Martin Chuzzlewit.

memes.label: David Crimple
memes.description: Secretary and resident Director of the Anglo-Bengalee Disinterested Loan and Life Assurance Company, whose name-change from "Crimp" to "Crimple" epitomizes the enterprise's cosmetic fraudulence.

relations.label: Anglo-Bengalee Disinterested Loan and Life Assurance Company

memes.label: Death
memes.description: The treatment of death and dying in Dickens's Martin Chuzzlewit — from Anthony's theatrical funeral to Jonas's violent end.

memes.label: Debt
memes.description: The network of financial obligations — both material and moral — that binds characters across Martin Chuzzlewit, from Jonas's crushing debts to the "debt of gratitude" owed by those who have received kindness.

memes.label: Deception
memes.description: The practice of deliberate falsehood in Martin Chuzzlewit — Pecksniff's defining method, but also a pervasive social phenomenon that extends from domestic fraud to the American land-swindle of Eden.

memes.label: "Dedication"
memes.description: "To **Miss Burdett Coutts** this tale is dedicated *with the true and earnest regard of* **the author**"

memes.label: Despair
memes.description: A pervading emotional register in Martin Chuzzlewit — Dickens charts despair from its comic uses through Pecksniff's theatrical self-pity to its darkest manifestation in Jonas Chuzzlewit's moral desolation, where despair and triumph merge into a single state.

memes.label: Doctor Jobling
memes.description: Medical officer of the Anglo-Bengalee Company, a physician whose professional rhetoric is as carefully curated as his wardrobe — and whose knowing distance from the company he serves betrays a shrewd understanding of its fraudulence.

relations.label: Anglo-Bengalee Disinterested Loan and Life Assurance Company

memes.label: Duty
memes.description: A moral language co-opted by hypocrites throughout Martin Chuzzlewit — duty is invoked by characters like Pecksniff to justify self-serving actions, while genuine duty is quietly performed by the novel's most virtuous figures.

memes.label: Eden
memes.description: The fraudulent American swampland "settlement" sold to Martin Chuzzlewit by the Eden Land Corporation, a pestilential swamp where he and Mark Tapley nearly die and where Martin's moral transformation begins.

memes.label: Education
memes.description: The theme of education in Martin Chuzzlewit, encompassing formal instruction, moral upbringing, and Dickens's critique of educational institutions and practices (P03-004).

memes.label: Elijah Pogram
memes.description: American Congressman and political orator encountered by Martin Chuzzlewit on his journey through the United States.

memes.label: Endurance
memes.description: Redirect to patience — the theme of cheerful endurance in Martin Chuzzlewit, embodied by Mark Tapley and Tom Pinch.

memes.label: Envy
memes.description: The corrosive desire for what others possess, dramatized in Charity Pecksniff's jealousy of her sister and the competitive grasping of the Chuzzlewit family in Dickens's novel.

memes.label: Family
memes.description: Family relationships in Martin Chuzzlewit, examining the Chuzzlewit dynasty's dysfunction and the Pinch siblings' redemptive bond.

memes.label: The Village Forge
memes.description: The village forge at Salisbury in Martin Chuzzlewit — a symbolic counterpoint to Pecksniff's architectural fraud, embodying honest labour, warmth, and the novel's moral geography (002-007).

memes.label: Forgiveness
memes.description: A moral language weaponized by hypocrites in Martin Chuzzlewit — Pecksniffian forgiveness is an act of aggression, while genuine reconciliation comes quietly, without rhetoric, in the novel's closing movement

memes.label: Fraud
memes.description: Redirect to deception — fraud in the context of Martin Chuzzlewit.

memes.label: Friendship
memes.description: Friendship as a moral force in Martin Chuzzlewit — Tom Pinch and John Westlock's bond, Mark Tapley's devotion, and the novel's critique of false fellowship.

memes.label: Furnival's Inn
memes.description: Furnival's Inn, High Holborn — John Westlock's London residence, a "shady, quiet place" that becomes a refuge for Tom Pinch and a symbol of friendship and renewal in Martin Chuzzlewit.

memes.label: Mr. Gander
memes.description: A boarder at Mrs. Todgers's commercial boarding-house, notable for his "witty turn" — originator of the "collars" joke about Bailey junior during the dinner party in Chapter IX. (009-110)

memes.label: General Choke
memes.description: American military figure and land speculator associated with the Eden Land Corporation.

memes.label: General Fladdock
memes.description: An American militia general who appears at the Norris family gathering in New York, embodying Dickens's satire of American military pretension and social ambition (017-078).

memes.label: Generosity
memes.description: The virtue of selfless giving and magnanimity, explored in Martin Chuzzlewit through contrasting characters from the generous Tom Pinch to the selfish Pecksniff.

memes.label: George Chuzzlewit
memes.description: A bachelor cousin in the Chuzzlewit family, memorable for his corpulence, his taste for overfeeding, and his sharp tongue at the family gathering in Chapter IV.

memes.label: Gratitude
memes.description: Gratitude as a defining moral virtue in Martin Chuzzlewit, embodied most fully in Tom Pinch — contrasted with Pecksniff's theatrical counterfeit and Martin Chuzzlewit's hard-won moral growth.

memes.label: Greed
memes.description: The destructive pursuit of wealth in Martin Chuzzlewit — the root of selfishness that drives the novel's plots of fraud, murder, and moral ruin from Anthony Chuzzlewit's cynical hoarding to Jonas's criminal schemes.

memes.label: Guilt
memes.description: The central moral-legal concept in Martin Chuzzlewit's crime plot — Dickens traces guilt from Jonas Chuzzlewit's murder through its psychological consequences, using "guilty" as both a legal charge and a state of mind that transforms the criminal from within.

memes.label: Hannibal Chollop
memes.description: A minor but memorable American character in Martin Chuzzlewit, described as "one of the most remarkable men in the country" — an armed, fever-proof frontiersman from Illinois who confronts Mark Tapley in Eden. (033-046)(033-066)

memes.label: Hatred
memes.description: The destructive passion that drives the novel's villains — from Jonas's murderous hatred of Tigg to Pecksniff's settled hatred of all who see through him — and the dark complement to the novel's celebration of loyalty and love.

memes.label: High Holborn
memes.description: The major London thoroughfare that connects several of the novel's key addresses — Mrs. Gamp's Kingsgate Street, John Westlock's Furnival's Inn, and the larger Holborn district that forms the commercial backdrop of the London chapters.

memes.label: Honesty
memes.description: A moral virtue that serves as a test of character in Martin Chuzzlewit, embodied by Tom Pinch and Tom's "ingenuous honesty" that disarms even strangers (003-095).

memes.label: Honour
memes.description: A concept systematically hollowed out by Dickens in Martin Chuzzlewit — from Mr. Pecksniff's weaponised appeals to "the honour of human nature" through Montague Tigg's promissory "sacred word of honour" to Tom Pinch's quiet, genuine honour that needs no declaration.

memes.label: Hospitality
memes.description: Hospitality as a social virtue and narrative device in Martin Chuzzlewit, centred on the Blue Dragon Inn.

memes.label: Humor
memes.description: Redirect to comedy — the comic and humorous elements in Martin Chuzzlewit.

memes.label: Hypocrisy
memes.description: The central satirical target of Martin Chuzzlewit — Dickens anatomises hypocrisy through Mr. Pecksniff, the novel's supreme hypocrite, whose moral rhetoric serves as a mask for the most calculating selfishness.

memes.label: Illness
memes.description: A recurring plot device and thematic element in Martin Chuzzlewit, used by Dickens to reveal character, advance the narrative, and explore vulnerability and dependence (003-006).

memes.label: Inheritance
memes.description: Redirect to wealth — the theme of inheritance and patrimony in Martin Chuzzlewit.

memes.label: Innocence
memes.description: The quality of moral guilelessness embodied by Tom Pinch, Ruth, and Mary Graham — a form of goodness that the novel's villains exploit but cannot ultimately corrupt.

memes.label: Islington
memes.description: A London suburb where Tom Pinch and his sister Ruth make their home, representing domestic happiness and modest independence in the novel's closing chapters (036-181).

memes.label: Mr. Jinkins
memes.description: A young architectural pupil boarding at Todgers's, known to his fellow boarders primarily through the comic sketches and jokes made at his expense.

memes.label: John Westlock
memes.description: Tom Pinch's loyal friend and the novel's quiet moral compass — a man of independent means who sees through Pecksniff from the start and whose steady decency is rewarded with marriage to Ruth Pinch.

memes.label: Jonas Chuzzlewit
memes.description: The violent, grasping branch of the Chuzzlewit family — a man raised on the principle of the main chance who graduates from petty cruelty to murder.

relations.label: Anglo-Bengalee Disinterested Loan and Life Assurance Company

memes.label: The Crimes of Jonas Chuzzlewit
memes.description: Jonas Chuzzlewit's criminal descent — from fraudulent scheming to the murder of Montague Tigg and his eventual suicide by poison — the novel's darkest plot thread and Dickens's study of moral degeneration.

memes.label: The Murder of Montague Tigg
memes.description: Jonas Chuzzlewit bludgeons Montague Tigg to death in a lonely wood to silence his blackmail, committing the novel's central crime.

memes.label: Justice
memes.description: The contested language of justice in Martin Chuzzlewit — a concept invoked by hypocrites to justify self-interest, while genuine justice is quietly performed by the novel's moral exemplars

memes.label: The Keyhole
memes.description: The keyhole as a recurring comic motif in Martin Chuzzlewit — an emblem of Pecksniff's surveillance, hypocrisy, and the novel's architecture of spying and being spied upon (002-011).

relations.label: Tom Pinch's discovery of Pecksniff's true character

memes.label: Kindness
memes.description: A central moral virtue in Martin Chuzzlewit, embodied most fully by Tom Pinch and serving as a moral yardstick against which other characters are measured (003-104).

memes.label: Kingsgate Street
memes.description: The London street off High Holborn where Mrs. Sarah Gamp maintains her residence — a modest address that serves as the headquarters of the novel's most memorably disreputable nurse.

memes.label: "Mrs. Gamp: 'Leave the bottle on the chimley-piece'"
memes.description: Mrs. Sarah Gamp's famous speech invoking her imaginary friend Mrs. Harris to justify placing a bottle of liquor "on the chimley-piece" while she nurses the dying — the novel's most quoted comic set-piece.
aliases: Leave the bottle on the chimley-piece

memes.label: Lewsome
memes.description: A medical practitioner and confidant of Jonas Chuzzlewit who becomes entangled in Jonas's criminal schemes.

memes.label: London
memes.description: The sprawling urban backdrop of Martin Chuzzlewit, a city of fog, commerce, crime, and concealment where Jonas Chuzzlewit's dark schemes unfold and characters converge in Dickens's characteristic metropolitan panorama.

memes.label: Love
memes.description: Love as a transformative force in Martin Chuzzlewit, operating through romantic devotion, familial affection, and selfless care.

memes.label: Loyalty
memes.description: The virtue of steadfast attachment in Martin Chuzzlewit — embodied by Tom Pinch, Mark Tapley, and Mary Graham, and set against the transactional relationships that dominate the novel's moral landscape.

memes.label: Major Pawkins
memes.description: American military figure encountered by Martin Chuzzlewit during his American travels.

memes.label: Mark Tapley
memes.description: Martin Chuzzlewit's cheerful servant and the novel's philosopher of jolliness — a man who measures his worth by his ability to remain good-humored under adversity.

memes.label: Marriage
memes.description: Marriage as social institution and plot device in Martin Chuzzlewit — a transaction weaponized by the selfish, a trap for the unwary, and in its rare genuine form, the novel's quiet reward for moral growth

memes.label: Martin and Mary's Wedding
memes.description: Redirect to marriage — the wedding of Martin Chuzzlewit Jr. and Mary Graham, the novel's romantic resolution (Chapter 54).

memes.label: Martin Chuzzlewit the Younger
memes.description: The protagonist of Martin Chuzzlewit — a selfish young man whose journey from England to America and through illness and hardship transforms him into a generous, self-aware adult.

memes.label: Martin Chuzzlewit Sr.
memes.description: The wealthy patriarch of the Chuzzlewit family — a man whose corrosive suspicion of selfishness in others becomes its own form of selfishness, and who orchestrates the novel's resolution through a long, elaborate deception of the hypocrite Pecksniff.

memes.label: Martin's Expulsion from Pecksniff's House
memes.description: Young Martin Chuzzlewit is denounced as a reprobate and cast out of Pecksniff's house, leading to his desperate resolution to seek his fortune in America.

memes.label: Martin's Journey to America
memes.description: Martin Chuzzlewit the Younger's voyage to America and his disastrous experience at the settlement of Eden — the novel's great satirical interlude and the crucible in which Martin's character is tested and transformed.

memes.label: Mary Graham
memes.description: The quiet heroine of Martin Chuzzlewit — old Martin's orphaned ward and companion, whose steadfast patience, self-forgetfulness, and constancy under trial make her the novel's model of unselfish love.

memes.label: Mercy Pecksniff
memes.description: The younger Pecksniff daughter, frivolous and laughing, who is forced into marriage with Jonas Chuzzlewit, endures his cruelty, and emerges widowed and sobered.

memes.label: Montague Tigg
memes.description: The con-man who rises from shabby-genteel beggary to president of the fraudulent Anglo-Bengalee Disinterested Loan and Life Assurance Company, only to be murdered by Jonas Chuzzlewit in a dark wood.

relations.label: Anglo-Bengalee Disinterested Loan and Life Assurance Company

memes.label: The Monument
memes.description: The Monument to the Great Fire of London, a recurring London landmark in Martin Chuzzlewit serving as a meeting place, a point of comic set-pieces, and a symbol of the city's endurance (008-062).

memes.label: Mortality
memes.description: Redirect to death — the theme of mortality in Martin Chuzzlewit.

memes.label: Mr. Bevan
memes.description: An honourable American gentleman who befriends Martin Chuzzlewit in New York, representing the best of American character in the novel.

memes.label: Mr. Fips
memes.description: A mysterious lawyer of Austin Friars, London — small, spare, and peaceable in black shorts and powder, who acts on anonymous instructions to employ Tom Pinch and whose eccentric manner masks a role in old Martin Chuzzlewit's benevolent design (039-109).

memes.label: Mr. Jefferson Brick
memes.description: American war correspondent and editor encountered by Martin Chuzzlewit, a caricature of sensationalist journalism.

memes.label: Mr. Jinkins
memes.description: The leading gentleman-boarder at Todgers's commercial boarding-house in London, noted for his superior talents, fashionable turn, and gift for oratory (008-069).

memes.label: Mr. La Fayette Kettle
memes.description: American newspaper editor encountered by Martin Chuzzlewit, a representative of the sensationalist American press.

memes.label: Mr. Mould
memes.description: An undertaker whose professional solemnity barely masks commercial satisfaction, embodying Dickens's satire of those who profit from death.

memes.label: Mr. Pecksniff
memes.description: Redirect to Seth Pecksniff, the fraudulent architect and central antagonist of Martin Chuzzlewit.

memes.label: "'Mr. Pecksniff was a moral man'"
memes.description: The novel's great satirical introduction of Seth Pecksniff — "Mr. Pecksniff was a moral man" — a passage that demonstrates Dickens's method of defining character through an accumulation of ironic praise.

memes.label: Mrs. Harris
memes.description: Redirect to Sarah Gamp — Mrs. Harris is Mrs. Gamp's imaginary friend in Martin Chuzzlewit.

memes.label: Mrs. Hominy
memes.description: American authoress and philosopher whose intellectual pretensions and patriotic fervor torment Martin Chuzzlewit on his journey.
aliases: the Mother of the Modern Gracchi

memes.label: Mrs. Lupin
memes.description: The warm-hearted landlady of the Blue Dragon inn at Salisbury, whose genuine hospitality contrasts with the calculated welcome of Mrs. Todgers's London boardinghouse.

memes.label: Mrs. Todgers
memes.description: Proprietress of Todgers's Commercial Boardinghouse in London, a key setting for the novel's London chapters.

memes.label: Mr. Nadgett
memes.description: The shadowy investigator employed by Montague Tigg at the Anglo-Bengalee, a man "born to be a secret" whose patient surveillance brings Jonas Chuzzlewit to justice.

relations.label: Anglo-Bengalee Disinterested Loan and Life Assurance Company

memes.label: National Hotel
memes.description: The raw, whitewashed American hotel where Martin Chuzzlewit and Mark Tapley stay before their disastrous journey to Eden — a symbol of democratic pretension and commercial hollowness in Dickens's American satire.

memes.label: New York
memes.description: The port city where Martin Chuzzlewit and Mark Tapley first arrive in America, depicted by Dickens as a hub of sensationalist journalism, social pretension, and commercial hustle.

memes.label: The Norris Family
memes.description: The Norris family of New York — anglophile socialites who host Martin Chuzzlewit in Chapter XVII, professing abolitionist principles while expressing casual racism and class snobbery. (017-069)

memes.label: Nursing
memes.description: The profession and practice of nursing as embodied in Sairey Gamp and Betsey Prig, Dickens's satirical portrait of pre-reform healthcare in Martin Chuzzlewit.

memes.label: Old Martin's Revelation
memes.description: Old Martin Chuzzlewit assembles the principal characters and publicly exposes Seth Pecksniff's hypocrisy before reconciling with his grandson.

memes.label: Patience
memes.description: Patience as a moral virtue in Martin Chuzzlewit — embodied by Tom Pinch's long-suffering loyalty and Mark Tapley's cheerful endurance, and contrasted with the novel's impatient schemers.

memes.label: The Pawnbroker
memes.description: The pawnbroker as a recurring site in Martin Chuzzlewit — the destination of Jonas Chuzzlewit's valuables and a register of his accelerating financial and moral descent (013-064).

relations.label: the Anglo-Bengalee Disinterested Loan and Life Assurance Company

memes.label: Mr. Pecksniff's House
memes.description: The Wiltshire residence of Seth Pecksniff near Salisbury, a house of sham respectability whose brazen door-plate proclaims "Pecksniff, Architect" and which serves as home to Tom Pinch and the Pecksniff daughters.

memes.label: Pecksniff's Architectural Practice
memes.description: Seth Pecksniff's fraudulent architectural practice near Salisbury — a school that teaches nothing, a profession that builds nothing, and Dickens's satire of institutional imposture.

memes.label: The Exposure of Pecksniff
memes.description: The climactic scene in which Old Martin Chuzzlewit publicly denounces Seth Pecksniff as a hypocrite, stripping him of wealth, position, and reputation before the assembled characters of the novel.

relations.label: Tom Pinch's Discovery of Pecksniff's Hypocrisy

memes.label: Pip
memes.description: A theatrical gentleman of Montague Tigg's acquaintance, a boisterous storyteller who appears at the dinner where Jonas Chuzzlewit is introduced to Tigg's disreputable circle (028-015).

memes.label: Poll Sweedlepipe
memes.description: A barber and bird-seller of Kingsgate Street, London — landlord to Mrs. Gamp and friend to Bailey, whose meek, bird-like nature makes him one of Dickens's most affectionately drawn comic portraits (026-003).

memes.label: "Postscript"
memes.description: "At a Public Dinner given to me on Saturday the 18th of April, 1868, in the city of New York, by two hundred representatives of the Press of the United States of"

memes.label: Poverty
memes.description: The treatment of poverty in Martin Chuzzlewit — from the genteel poverty of Tom Pinch to the utter destitution that threatens Martin in America, and the moral economy Dickens constructs around wealth and want (003-111).

memes.label: "Preface"
memes.description: "What is exaggeration to one class of minds and perceptions, is plain truth to another. That which is commonly called a long-sight, perceives in a prospect innum"

memes.label: Pride
memes.description: A pervasive moral failing in Martin Chuzzlewit — Dickens examines pride as the Chuzzlewit family's ancestral weakness, a corrosive force that isolates individuals from genuine human connection and feeds the larger pattern of selfishness.

memes.label: Redemption
memes.description: The possibility of moral transformation and forgiveness in Martin Chuzzlewit — experienced chiefly by young Martin through suffering in America and by old Martin through his final unmasking of Pecksniff.

memes.label: Remorse
memes.description: A moral emotion conspicuously absent in Martin Chuzzlewit — Dickens traces remorse as the dividing line between redeemable and irredeemable characters, with Jonas Chuzzlewit's inability to feel it marking his final descent.

memes.label: Revenge
memes.description: Revenge as a driving passion in Martin Chuzzlewit — from Jonas Chuzzlewit's murderous vengeance to the novel's moral argument against retaliatory justice (047-052).

memes.label: Ruth Pinch
memes.description: Tom Pinch's devoted younger sister, a governess who embodies Dickens's ideal of cheerful, self-sacrificing domestic womanhood — the moral heart of the novel's Salisbury scenes.

memes.label: Salisbury
memes.description: The cathedral town of Salisbury in Wiltshire, near which Mr. Pecksniff's house and the village of the Blue Dragon are situated — the novel's initial English setting and a recurring point of arrival and departure.

memes.label: Sarah Gamp
memes.description: The alcoholic nurse-midwife and Dickens's most memorable comic grotesque — Mrs. Sarah Gamp, whose umbrella became synonymous with disreputable nursing and whose imaginary friend Mrs. Harris served as witness to her every self-serving claim.

memes.label: Satire
memes.description: Redirect to comedy — Dickens's satirical method in Martin Chuzzlewit.

memes.label: Secrets and Surveillance
memes.description: The motif of secrecy, eavesdropping, and surveillance in Martin Chuzzlewit as both a plot mechanism and a moral commentary.

memes.label: Selfishness
memes.description: The central moral theme of Martin Chuzzlewit — Dickens's sustained examination of selfishness as the root of all human vice, embodied and confronted by characters across the novel.

memes.label: Seth Pecksniff
memes.description: The arch-hypocrite of Martin Chuzzlewit — a moralizing architect who builds nothing and exploits everyone, serving as Dickens's supreme study of selfishness disguised as virtue.

memes.label: Shame
memes.description: A social and moral emotion that Dickens uses to distinguish character in Martin Chuzzlewit — shame marks the boundary between Pecksniff's shamelessness, old Martin's wounded pride, and Jonas Chuzzlewit's ultimate degradation where shame, guilt, and cowardice collide.

memes.label: Spottletoe
memes.description: Mr. and Mrs. Spottletoe — Chuzzlewit relatives who descend upon the Dragon Inn to contest old Martin's favour; Mrs. Spottletoe is "Chuzzlewit's own niece" and Mr. Spottletoe a bald, fiery man with enormous whiskers whose indignation is as prodigious as it is ineffectual (004-049).

memes.label: Surveillance
memes.description: The act of watching and being watched in Martin Chuzzlewit, from Nadgett the detective's shadowing of Jonas to the social surveillance of reputation that pervades Dickens's novel.

memes.label: Suspicion
memes.description: A defining trait of several key characters in Martin Chuzzlewit — suspicion operates as the protective armour of the selfish, isolating the suspicious from genuine love and making them vulnerable to the very deception they fear.

memes.label: Tacker
memes.description: Mr. Mould's experienced assistant and foreman at the undertaker's establishment, a man whose professional knowledge of funeral arrangements commands respect even from his master (019-081).

memes.label: The Temple
memes.description: The Inner Temple, a legal district in London where Tom Pinch finds employment with Mr. Fips and where key events of the novel's later chapters unfold (039-132).

memes.label: The Anglo-Bengalee
memes.description: Redirect to the Anglo-Bengalee Disinterested Loan and Life Assurance Company, the fraudulent financial institution run by Montague Tigg in Martin Chuzzlewit.

memes.label: The Blue Dragon
memes.description: The village inn near Salisbury where Mrs. Lupin presides, Mark Tapley works, and key meetings between the novel's central characters occur.

memes.label: The Eden Disaster
memes.description: Martin Chuzzlewit and Mark Tapley arrive at the fraudulent "Eden" settlement, fall gravely ill, and nearly die before escaping back to England.

memes.label: The Resolution of the Will
memes.description: The final settlement in which Old Martin Chuzzlewit reveals his true will — restoring Martin and Mary to his favour, blessing their marriage, and establishing Tom Pinch as the novel's moral beneficiary — bringing the inheritance plot to its redemptive conclusion.

relations.label: The Wedding of Martin and Mary

memes.label: The Rowdy Journal
memes.description: The New York Rowdy Journal, edited by Colonel Diver, is Dickens's savage satire of American journalism — a newspaper that substitutes vituperation for truth and bombast for integrity.

memes.label: The Watertoast Association
memes.description: The Watertoast Association, a fictional American civic organization in Martin Chuzzlewit, through which Dickens satirizes the bombastic rhetoric and hollow moral posturing of American public life.

memes.label: Todgers's
memes.description: Mrs. Todgers's Commercial Boarding-House in London, a singularly labyrinthine establishment near the Monument where the Pecksniffs stay and key urban scenes unfold.

memes.label: Tom Pinch
memes.description: The novel's moral center — Pecksniff's exploited assistant whose goodness, simplicity, and eventual disillusionment embody Dickens's argument for the superiority of generous selflessness.

memes.label: Tom Pinch's Discovery of Pecksniff's Hypocrisy
memes.description: Tom Pinch overhears Mary Graham reveal Pecksniff's true character in the village church, shattering his lifelong idolisation of his master.
aliases: Tom learns the truth about Pecksniff

memes.label: Trust
memes.description: The broken and restored currency of trust in Martin Chuzzlewit — Dickens traces how suspicion propagates itself, making trust both the novel's scarcest resource and its ultimate reward

memes.label: The Undertaker
memes.description: The figure of the undertaker in Martin Chuzzlewit — Mr. Mould's professional performance of grief, Dickens's satire of the commerce of death, and the novel's anatomy of the professions that profit from mortality (019-003).

memes.label: Wealth
memes.description: Wealth and the pursuit of money as the central thematic engine of Martin Chuzzlewit — the force that drives the family's scheming, shapes character, and serves as Dickens's instrument for moral judgment.

relations.label: Anglo-Bengalee Disinterested Loan and Life Assurance Company

memes.label: Wolf
memes.description: A literary gentleman and journal editor of Montague Tigg's acquaintance, the author of "warm" satirical articles who appears at the dinner introducing Jonas Chuzzlewit to Tigg's London circle (028-015).

memes.label: Zedekiah Scadder
memes.description: American land speculator who sells Martin Chuzzlewit a worthless plot in the fraudulent settlement of Eden.

memes.label: Zephaniah Scadder
memes.description: The one-eyed American land agent who sells Martin Chuzzlewit a fraudulent stake in the Eden settlement, embodying Dickens's most savage satire of American commercial dishonesty (021-115).
